V1_Baseline.title: Prophet Goes S.Platt!!! Comic Book
V1_Baseline.historicalContext: The comic book appears to be from the 1990s or early 2000s, based on the art style and cover design. The title 'Prophet Goes S.Platt!!!' suggests a crossover or special edition featuring the artist S. Platt.
V1_Baseline.collectorSignificance: The comic's rarity depends on the specific issue and print run. The presence of S. Platt's name could increase its value, especially if it's a signed edition (though no signature is visible in the image). The condition of the comic (unseen) is a major factor in determining its value. It appears to be in a protective sleeve.
V1_Baseline.valuation: $5 - $20, depending on the specific issue, condition, and whether it is signed. Without more information, it is difficult to provide a more precise estimate.

V2_Handwriting.title: Prophet Goes S.Platt Comic Book

V1_Baseline.title: Denver and Rio Grande Railway Freight Receipt, 1883
V1_Baseline.cleanedTranscription:
FORM 200.-0-10-82-100M
FREIGHT AGENT'S OFFICE,
No.
Denver and Rio Grande Railway.
Seby 5 1883
Received from Hett, Touckine to D.A. Belden
in apparently good order, (contents and value unknown,) consigned to
Red Cliff
State of Cole
the following described property.
marked as noted in margin, to be
and delivered in like good
transported over the line of the Railway to the Company's Freight Station at
order to the consignee or owner, AT SAID STATION, or to such Company or Carrier (if the property is to be forwarded beyond said
Station') whose line may be considered a part of the route to the place of destination of said goods or packages; it being distinctly
understood that the responsibility of this Company as a common carrier shall cease at the Station where delivered to such Company
or carrier; but it guarantees that the rate of freight for the transportation of said property from the place of shipment to the place
swhere consigned, shall not exceed
per
and charges advanced by this Company,
UPON THE FOLLOWING CONDITIONS:
This Receipt to be presented without Alteration or Erasure.
AS CONSIGNED MARKS. DESCRIPTION OF ARTICLES.
D.D. Belden 15 Box Candles
R Cliff
Cols
Rates.
Special per
Amount Charges Advanced, $

Agent
V1_Baseline.historicalContext: This document is a freight receipt from the Denver and Rio Grande Railway, dated February 5, 1883. It details a shipment from 'Hett, Touckine' to 'D.A. Belden' of 'Box Candles' to 'Red Cliff', Colorado. The Denver and Rio Grande Railway was a prominent railway company in the late 19th century, crucial for transporting goods and people across the mountainous regions of Colorado and the American West. The form number '200.-0-10-82-100M' suggests a standardized form used by the railway. The mention of 'Seby 5' is likely a misspelling of 'Feby 5', indicating February 5th.
V1_Baseline.collectorSignificance: This freight receipt is a relatively rare piece of ephemera from a significant railway company. The presence of handwritten details, including the consignee, consignor, and description of goods, adds to its historical interest. The signature of the agent enhances its value. The condition appears to be good, though there are some creases and possible discoloration. The presence of price stickers suggests it was previously offered for sale.
V1_Baseline.valuation: $35 - $75. The value is based on its age, historical significance, the railway company's importance, and the presence of handwritten details and a signature. The condition and the fact that it is framed also contribute to the value. Similar railway ephemera can fetch prices in this range, depending on condition and specific details.

V2_Handwriting.historicalContext: This document is a freight receipt from the Denver and Rio Grande Railway, dated February 5, 1883. It originates from Denver, Colorado, and reflects the transportation of goods (a box of candles) to D.D. Belden & Cliff in Colo. The form number suggests it was a standardized document used by the railway company. The text indicates the common carrier responsibilities and limitations of the railway at the time.
V2_Handwriting.collectorSignificance: The document is a tangible piece of railroad history, specifically related to the Denver and Rio Grande Railway, a significant player in the development of the American West. The presence of handwritten details, including the consignee, destination, and description of goods, adds to its uniqueness. The condition appears to be fair, given its age. The presence of a signature (likely the agent) enhances its collectibility.
V2_Handwriting.valuation: $25 - $50. The value is based on its age, historical significance as a railroad document, and the presence of handwritten details. The condition and the specific railway company involved also influence the price. The presence of the price tag suggests it was previously offered for $15.

V1_Baseline.title: Delta Mercantile Co. Letter, September 10, 1890
V1_Baseline.cleanedTranscription:
THE DELTA MERCANTILE CO.,
(Successors to MCGRAHANAR & BUTLER,)
-DEALERS IN-
General Merchandise.

GEO. STEPHAN, Pres't
A. C. BUTLER, Vice-Pres't
FRANKE. DODGE, Sec'y and Treas.

Delta, Colo., Sep 10 1890

S.V.R.G.
Cypress


Gentlemen

Can you give us
a Special rate on Laundry
Proper Shipped from this People
to Arvour Laundries & return
What rate Can you give us
Awaiting your Earliest Convenience
Ite remain your Props

THE DELTA MERCANTILE CO
Per. M Stephan
V1_Baseline.historicalContext: This is a letter from The Delta Mercantile Co. in Delta, Colorado, dated September 10, 1890. The letter is addressed to S.V.R.G. Cypress, likely a business or individual involved in laundry services. The Delta Mercantile Co. is inquiring about special rates for shipping laundry to Arvour Laundries and back. The letterhead indicates that the company was a successor to McGrahanar & Butler and dealt in general merchandise. The officers of the company are listed as Geo. Stephan (President), A.C. Butler (Vice-President), and Franke Dodge (Secretary and Treasurer).
V1_Baseline.collectorSignificance: The letter is a tangible piece of late 19th-century commercial correspondence. The presence of a printed letterhead with the company's name, officers, and business type adds to its historical value. The handwritten content provides insight into the business practices of the time. The signature of 'M Stephan' adds a personal touch. The condition appears to be good, though there is some fading and wear consistent with age. The fact that it is framed suggests it was considered valuable enough to preserve.
V1_Baseline.valuation: $25 - $45. The value is based on its age, historical context, and the presence of a printed letterhead and handwritten content. The good condition and framing enhance its value. The specific content related to laundry services might appeal to collectors interested in the history of that industry or the local history of Delta, Colorado.

The letterhead indicates that Delta Mercantile Co. was the successor to McGrahanar & Butler and dealt in general merchandise. The letter is signed by Geo Stephan, President.
V2_Handwriting.collectorSignificance: The letter is a tangible piece of late 19th-century commercial correspondence. The letterhead provides information about the company's history and business. The handwriting is typical of the period. The presence of the company name and the signature of the president adds to its historical value. The condition appears to be good, though there is some fading and wear. The framing may add to the value.
V2_Handwriting.valuation: $25 - $50. The value is based on the age, historical content, and the presence of a company letterhead and signature. The condition and framing also contribute to the value. The price tag of $15.00 suggests a lower bound, but the historical significance could justify a higher price.
